name: Universal Robots API (URScript / RTDE)
what_it_does: SDK and protocol interfaces (URScript, RTDE, Dashboard Server) for programming and monitoring Universal Robots collaborative robot arms (UR3e through UR20) for industrial automation.
use_cases: Programmatically moving robot arm to specific waypoints for assembly or pick-and-place
Reading real-time joint angles, force/torque, and I/O state for monitoring agents
Uploading and triggering URScript programs remotely via Dashboard Server API
Integrating cobot actions into multi-step manufacturing workflow agents
Collecting telemetry data for predictive maintenance analysis
not_for: Non-UR robots (API is proprietary to Universal Robots hardware)
Rapid prototyping without industrial robotics expertise
Remote/cloud control without on-premise hardware and network access
best_when: An agent needs to control industrial cobot arms for manufacturing, lab automation, or quality control tasks.
avoid_when: You need mobile robotics, a cross-platform abstraction layer, or consumer robot hardware.

auth.notes: TCP/IP connection over local network to robot controller. Dashboard Server on port 29999, RTDE on 30004, Primary Interface on 30001. No cryptographic auth - network access control is the security boundary.

pricing.free_tier_limits: All SDKs and protocol documentation are free; requires UR hardware
pricing.paid_tiers: UR3e ~$35k, UR5e ~$45k, UR10e ~$55k, UR20 ~$75k (approximate list prices)
pricing.notes: Universal Robots+ marketplace has commercial apps/plugins, but core API is free.

performance.rate_limits: RTDE: 125-500Hz; Dashboard Server: command-response, no explicit limit

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: UR documentation is comprehensive for industrial automation engineers but assumes deep domain knowledge. Error codes are well-documented in the controller manual.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Move commands are not idempotent in that repeated execution moves the robot to the same position, but concurrent execution must be sequenced carefully. Waypoint-based programming is repeatable.
known_agent_gotchas: Robot must be in 'Remote Control' mode before accepting external commands
Safety limits must be configured for the workspace before deployment
URScript programs execute synchronously - async execution requires careful state management
RTDE data must be set up with a configuration file before starting the session
E-stop state must be cleared manually and cannot be reset programmatically in all scenarios

security_score_components.security_notes: No TLS or authentication on control interfaces. Designed for isolated industrial networks. Network segmentation via VLAN or air-gap is mandatory for production safety.
